aliases: Islamic State of Iraq and Syria
Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
description: Transnational Salafi jihadist militant organization and unrecognized quasi-state that proclaimed a worldwide caliphate in 2014. Originating from al-Qaeda in Iraq, it controlled large territories in Iraq and Syria from 2013 to 2019, enforcing extremist Islamic law. Known for brutal violence, including genocides, beheadings, and terrorist attacks globally. After losing its Middle Eastern territories, it reverted to insurgency and maintains significant presence in Africa.

country_links.evidence_quote: By June 2015, IS had also established official "provinces" in Libya, Egypt (Sinai Peninsula), Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Algeria, Afghanistan, Pakistan, Nigeria and the North Caucasus.
country_links.context: IS has an official province in Afghanistan and its affiliate ISIS-K operates there.

country_links.evidence_quote: 13 November 2014 : Claim of territory in Libya, Egypt, Algeria, Saudi Arabia, Yemen
country_links.context: The Islamic State claimed territory in Algeria.

country_links.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Azerbaijan Province (IS-AP) emerged in 2019 and operates in Azerbaijan .
country_links.context: IS has an Azerbaijan Province that operates in Azerbaijan.

country_links.context: IS's Bengal Province is active in Bangladesh.
